text.title: Employer Branding Agent Rules
text.summary: Human-approval gates, ToS constraints, and antipatterns for employer branding agent workflows.

rule.statement: Any external publication — LinkedIn post, Glassdoor response, Instagram content, blog post — requires human approval before publish. Agents draft; humans approve and post.
rule.rationale: Brand voice drift, DEI misrepresentation, and legally risky compensation claims are impossible to fully prevent at generation time. The approval gate is the only reliable safeguard.

rule.statement: DEI claims (diversity percentages, ERG names, inclusion programs) must be sourced from HRIS data, not synthesized. Every DEI claim in a draft must have a data citation or be marked UNVERIFIED.
rule.rationale: Invented diversity statistics are a serious legal and reputational risk. Candidates and journalists verify claims; inaccurate statistics cause brand damage that exceeds the original benefit.

rule.statement: Do not scrape Glassdoor or Indeed without their official API (Glassdoor for Employers tier). Automated scraping without API access violates ToS and can result in employer account suspension.
rule.rationale: A suspended employer account on Glassdoor removes all company review responses and brand profile, causing permanent brand damage that exceeds the value of scraped data.

antipattern.description: Cliché injection in every brand draft: "passionate team," "world-class culture," "amazing people."
antipattern.reason: These phrases appear on 90%+ of employer brand pages and provide zero differentiation. They train candidates to ignore the content.

antipattern.description: Generating fake employee names or composite quotes when raw material is missing.
antipattern.reason: Fabricated employee voices are unethical and constitute false advertising in candidate-facing materials. Always require real employee input before drafting.

antipattern.description: Brand voice drift across 50+ content generations without re-anchoring.
antipattern.reason: Agents drift from the established voice even when the brand guide is in context. Include 3 approved sample posts in every content generation prompt to re-anchor tone.
